zh.Stratford-upon-Avon, Warwickshire, Engla: 英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾史達科特 奧 阿沃
zh.lifeSummary: 當Henry Alexander Thomas de Richmond Webb於1350年5月15日出生在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾史達科特 奧 阿沃時，他的父親Richard de Richmond Webb8歲，母親Elizabeth de Burgh 4th Countess of Ulster17歲。他於1371年與Alice Julienne Cloward在英國瓦維其可爾史達科特 奧 阿沃結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1397年1月1日在他的家鄉享年46歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Henry Alexander Thomas de Richmond Webb于1350年5月15日出生在英国英格兰瓦維其可爾史達科特 奧 阿沃时，他的父亲Richard de Richmond Webb8岁，母亲Elizabeth de Burgh 4th Countess of Ulster17岁。他于1371年与Alice Julienne Cloward在英国瓦維其可爾史達科特 奧 阿沃结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1397年1月1日在他的家乡享年46岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Richmond
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from Richmond (North Yorkshire). The placename was probably transferred after the Norman Conquest from any of numerous places in France named with Old French riche ‘mighty, strong’ + munt, mont ‘mountain, hill’. Richmond in southwest London received this name only in the reign of Henry VII, in honor of the king, who had been Earl of Richmond until he came to the throne, and is unlikely to be the source of this surname.
occasionally a variant of Richman , with excrescent -d. The two names were probably often confused.
English:

variants.name: Richman
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Richeman, either from an unrecorded Old English Rīcmann, or from a pet form of Richard + the hypocoristic suffix -man. The name was sometimes interchangeable with Rickman , especially in recent times.
English: occasionally perhaps a nickname from Middle English riche ‘rich, powerful’ + -man. Compare Rich .
English: perhaps a variant of   Richmond  , or vice versa.

nameMeanings.name: Webb
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a weaver, from early Middle English webbe (Old English webba (masculine) or webbe (feminine), probably used of both male and female weavers). This word survived into Middle English long enough to give rise to the surname, but was already obsolescent as an agent noun; hence the secondary forms with the agent suffixes -er and -ster (see Webster , Webber and compare Weaver ).
Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish (Ashkenazic) surnames, cognates of 1, including Weber and Weberman.
History: Richard Webb, a Lowland Scot, was an admitted freeman of Boston in 1632, and in 1635 was one of the first settlers of Hartford, CT.

variants.name: Webster
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name for a weaver, from Middle English webbester ‘weaver’ (Old English webbestre ‘female weaver’). By the time of surname formation, the gender distinction of the -stre suffix had almost completely disappeared. Compare Webb , Webber , and Weaver .

History: The name Webster was brought to North America from England independently by several different bearers in the 17th and 18th centuries. One John Webster settled in Ipswich, MA, in 1635; another John Webster (died 1661), ancestor of the lexicographer Noah Webster, emigrated to Cambridge, MA, c. 1631 and later became one of the founders of the colony of CT, of which he was appointed governor in 1656. Daniel Webster (1782–1852), politician and orator, was born in Salisbury, NH, a descendant of Thomas Webster, a prominent 17th-century citizen of Ipswich, MA, whose family had settled there around 1635, while he was still a child.

variants.name: Web
meanings: English: variant of   Webb  .

variants.name: Webber
meanings: English (Devon and Somerset): occupational name for a weaver, from Middle English webber, an agent derivative of Webb .
Americanized form of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Weber   ‘weaver’.

variants.name: Webbe
meanings: English: variant of   Webb  .

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

nameMeanings.name: Alexander
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

en.lifeSummary: When Henry Alexander Thomas de Richmond Webb was born on 15 May 1350, in Stratford on Avon, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Richard de Richmond Webb, was 8 and his mother, Elizabeth de Burgh 4th Countess of Ulster, was 17. He married Alice Julienne Cloward in 1371, in Stratford on Avon, Warwickshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 1 January 1397, in his hometown, at the age of 46.

ja.lifeSummary: Henry Alexander Thomas de Richmond Webbが1350年5月15日にイギリスイギリスワーウィクシュアイアストラトフォード オン アボンで生まれた時，父Richard de Richmond Webbは8歳，母Elizabeth de Burgh 4th Countess of Ulsterは17歳であった。1371年にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアストラトフォード オン アボンでAlice Julienne Clowardと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1397年1月1日に故郷で46歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Henry Alexander Thomas de Richmond Webb(이)가 1350년 5월 15일 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 스트라트퍼드  아폰에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard de Richmond Webb(은)는 8세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth de Burgh 4th Countess of Ulster(은)는 17세였다. 그는 1371년 영국 워윅실르 스트라트퍼드  아폰에서 Alice Julienne Cloward(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1397년 1월 1일 그의 고향에서 46세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Henry Alexander Thomas de Richmond Webb родился 15 мая 1350 г. в местности стратфорд он авон, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Richard de Richmond Webb было 8 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth de Burgh 4th Countess of Ulster, 17. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice Julienne Cloward в 1371 г. в местности стратфорд он авон, варвиккшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 1 января 1397 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 46 лет.
